title: quercus phellos var. pumila
intro: Quercus phellos var. pumila, also known as dwarf willow oak or shrubby willow oak, is a small to medium-sized deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Quercus phellos species. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to provide health benefits like reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Quercus phellos var. pumila include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or topically to leverage its medicinal properties.
